workflow.name: Introduce the causal leverage card harness and demonstrate it on the committed card set

pre_registration.rationale: Harness module is sealed at commit 69fd5ff before this card's measurement is taken. The card was authored in the same session as its measurement, so the seal is on the harness toolchain rather than on a separate draft of this card. Future prospective cards should seal the card draft itself.

objective.statement: Demonstrate that the leverage-card/v1 harness can score at least three independently-authored cards without FAIL, with at least one card scoring PASS, and that each card emits a foundations-compatible evidence payload.
objective.metric_definition: Count of committed leverage cards under memory/research/leverage/ whose `python3 scripts/run_leverage_card.py <card> --emit-evidence` exit code is 0 and whose foundations-validator decision on the emitted payload is not FAIL.

baseline.name: zero leverage cards, no harness
baseline.source: git history prior to commit 69fd5ff; T-018 matrix row at REVIEW with `causal leverage card benchmark (to add)` in the validation column

intervention.description: Ship the leverage-card/v1 harness (module, CLI, unit tests), the protocol doc, and three independently-authored cards: a retrospective attestation of T-015 (Kerr), a retrospective attestation of T-012 (hyperstition robustness), and this prospective card on T-018 itself.

denominators.human_effort: single session (~1 working day) to ship harness, doc, and three cards
denominators.evidence_cost: low — deterministic scorer + foundations validator round-trip

reversibility.rollback_method: git revert of commits 69fd5ff..HEAD on feat/causal-leverage-card-harness branch
reversibility.blast_radius: sandy-chaos repo only; no external systems touched

failure_conditions: Any committed card under memory/research/leverage/ scores FAIL → harness rejects its own corpus
Fewer than three cards land in this commit set → threshold not met
Foundations validator FAILs any emitted evidence payload → harness schema diverges from FOUNDATIONS marker policy
Unit test suite reports failures → harness implementation regression
Retrospective discipline is bypassed (a retrospective PASS card without attestation disclosure scores PASS) → schema is not actually enforcing the rule

observations.note: 17 unit tests pass on harness commit 69fd5ff

observations.condition: foundations-validator round-trip on each evidence payload

measured_outcome.summary: Three independently-authored leverage cards (kerr attestation, hyperstition robustness attestation, harness introduction) all score without FAIL under the harness. Each emits a foundations-compatible evidence payload that validate_foundations.py accepts. T-018 metric satisfied: 3 cards scoring non-FAIL, threshold 3.

decision.rationale: Harness ships with 17 passing unit tests, three scoring cards, and clean foundations-validator round-trips. PASS is conditional and self-referential: the harness is scoring its own introduction card. Per docs/22 protocol, T-018 may move to PASS with an explicit `next_test` for an independent prospective card on an unrelated workflow.
